instruction:
We now define an algorithm: The definition of a(n) is the chromatic number (or Heawood number) Chi(n) of a surface of genus n, which represents the minimum number of colors required to color a map on that surface so that no two adjacent regions share the same color. Given the input x_list (a series of values): [60, 61, 62, 63, 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69], determine the corresponding output sequence y_list.

Options:
A. [30, 30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32, 32, 33]
B. [30, 30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32]
C. [30, 30, 30, 30, 30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 31]
D. [31, 31, 30, 30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 31, 32]
E. [30, 31, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32, 32, 32, 32]
F. [31, 31, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32, 32, 32, 33]
G. [29, 29, 30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32]
H. [30, 30, 31, 31, 32, 32, 32, 32, 33, 33]
I. [30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32, 32, 32]
J. [30, 30, 31, 31, 31, 32, 32, 32, 32, 33]
